2604.24082.title: Jailbreaking Frontier Foundation Models Through Intention Deception
2604.24082.paper_summary: Large (vision-)language models exhibit remarkable capability but remain highly susceptible to jailbreaking. Existing safety training approaches aim to have the model learn a refusal boundary between safe and unsafe, based on the user's intent. It has been found that this binary training regime often leads to brittleness, since the user intent cannot reliably be evaluated, especially if the attacker obfuscates their intent, and also makes the system seem unhelpful. In response, frontier models, such as GPT-5, have shifted from refusal-based safeguards to safe completion, that aims to maximize helpfulness while obeying safety constraints. However, safe completion could be exploited when a user pretends their intention is benign. Specifically, this intent inversion would be effective in multi-turn conversation, where the attacker has multiple opportunities to reinforce their deceptively benign intent. In this work, we introduce a novel multi-turn jailbreaking method that exploits this vulnerability. Our approach gradually builds conversational trust by simulating benign-seeming intentions and by exploiting the consistency property of the model, ultimately guiding the target model toward harmful, detailed outputs. Most crucially, our approach also uncovered an additional class of model vulnerability that we call para-jailbreaking that has been unnoticed up to now. Para-jailbreaking describes the situation where the model may not reveal harmful direct reply to the attack query, however the information that it reveals is nevertheless harmful. Our contributions are threefold. First, it achieves high success rates against frontier models including GPT-5-thinking and Claude-Sonnet-4.5. Second, our approach revealed and addressed para-jailbreaking harmful output. Third, experiments on multimodal VLM models showed that our approach outperformed state-of-the-art models.
2604.24082.paper_summary_zh: 大型（視覺）語言模型展現出卓越的能力，但仍然對越獄攻擊高度敏感。現有的安全訓練方法旨在讓模型根據用戶的意圖學習安全與不安全之間的拒絕邊界。研究發現，這種二元訓練模式往往導致脆弱性，因為用戶意圖無法可靠地評估，特別是當攻擊者模糊其意圖時，並且還使系統顯得不夠有幫助。作為回應，前沿模型，如GPT-5，已經從基於拒絕的安全措施轉向安全完成，旨在在遵循安全約束的同時最大化有用性。然而，當用戶假裝其意圖是良性時，安全完成可能會被利用。具體而言，這種意圖反轉在多輪對話中會有效，因為攻擊者有多次機會來強化他們的欺騙性良性意圖。在這項工作中，我們介紹了一種新穎的多輪越獄方法，利用了這一脆弱性。我們的方法通過模擬看似良性的意圖並利用模型的一致性特性，逐步建立對話信任，最終引導目標模型產生有害且詳細的輸出。最關鍵的是，我們的方法還揭示了一種額外的模型脆弱性類別，我們稱之為para-jailbreaking，這在之前一直未被注意。Para-jailbreaking描述了這樣一種情況：模型可能不會對攻擊查詢揭示有害的直接回覆，然而它揭示的信息仍然是有害的。我們的貢獻有三方面。首先，它在包括GPT-5-thinking和Claude-Sonnet-4.5在內的前沿模型中實現了高成功率。其次，我們的方法揭示並解決了para-jailbreaking的有害輸出。第三，對多模態VLM模型的實驗顯示，我們的方法超越了最先進的模型。
2604.24082.author: Xinhe Wang et.al.
2604.24082.authors: Xinhe Wang, Katia Sycara, Yaqi Xie
